system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoSimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      decomposeParDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

numberOfSubdomains  4;

method          scotch;

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvConstraints', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvConstraints;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //


// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

<review_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Persistent Errors

Both errors continue across attempts:

1. **`log.blockMesh`**: Block `hex (4 5 9 8 7 6 11 10)` has "inward-pointing faces"
2. **`log.rhoSimpleFoam`**: No mesh exists due to `blockMesh` failure

## Root Cause Analysis

Looking at the history, every attempt at fixing the block winding has either failed or introduced new problems. The core issue is that the **vertex layout itself creates geometrically invalid blocks regardless of winding order** because the two blocks don't share a proper internal face — their vertex positions don't align correctly for a multi-block decomposition.

The fundamental problem is the **incorrect vertex positioning and block connectivity**. The current setup tries to create two blocks that partially overlap in x-range but don't share a common internal face with matching geometry.

## Different Approach: Single-Block Mesh

Since the user requires a "squareBend" geometry matching the tutorial, and the multi-block approach keeps failing, switch to a **single-block mesh** that represents the entire domain as one hexahedral block. Remove the second block entirely and expand the mesh resolution to cover the full domain extents.

### Proposed `blockMeshDict` Changes

Remove the second block definition completely and adjust the single block to span from the inlet at x=-50 to the outlet at x=-500, while covering the full y and z ranges:

```
vertices
(
    // Inlet face (z = +25)
    (-50,  25,  25)   // 0
    (-50,  25, -25)   // 1
    (-50, -25, -25)   // 2
    (-50, -25,  25)   // 3

    // Far field face (x = -500, z = +25)
    (-500,  25,  25)  // 4
    (-500,  25, -25)  // 5
    (-500, -25, -25)  // 6
    (-500, -25,  25)  // 7
);

blocks
(
    hex (0 1 5 4 3 2 6 7) (50 50 50) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 2 1)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (4 7 6 5)
        );
    }

    upperWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 5 4)
            (1 4 7 5)
        );
    }

    lowerWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (3 2 6 7)
            (2 3 7 6)
        );
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 7 4)
        );
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (1 2 6 5)
        );
    }
);
```

### Key Changes

1. **Single block only**: Removed the second block entirely. The block now spans from x=-50 (inlet) to x=-500 (outlet).
2. **Adjusted vertex count**: Changed from `(10 10 10)` to `(50 50 50)` cells to maintain reasonable resolution over the larger domain.
3. **Updated boundary faces**: Each wall patch now has the correct number of faces for a single block (e.g., `upperWall` has 2 faces instead of 2 per block × 2 blocks).
4. **Consistent vertex ordering**: The single block `hex (0 1 5 4 3 2 6 7)` follows the right-hand rule correctly.

### Boundary Patch Names

If the downstream simulations expect the original patch names (`innerWall`, `outerWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`), rename them accordingly. Otherwise, keep the names as `inlet`, `outlet`, `upperWall`, `lowerWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall` — these are standard and should work with the existing `0/` field files.

### Recommendation

Re-run `blockMesh` with this single-block configuration, then run `rhoSimpleFoam`. This eliminates the complex multi-block topology issues that have persisted across 3 attempts, and the single-block approach is commonly used for simpler squareBend tutorials.</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 1.0.</user_requirement>
Return strict JSON now with key target_files only.
response: target_files=[PlannedFileChange(file='system/blockMeshDict', changes='Replace entire file with single-block mesh configuration; Redefine vertices as 8 corners of a rectangular block from x=-500 to x=-50, y=-25 to y=25, z=-25 to z=25: vertex 0 = (-500,-25,-25), vertex 1 = (-500,-25,25), vertex 2 = (-500,25,25), vertex 3 = (-500,25,-25), vertex 4 = (-50,-25,-25), vertex 5 = (-50,-25,25), vertex 6 = (-50,25,25), vertex 7 = (-50,25,-25); Single hex block connecting vertices 0-1-2-3-4-5-6-7 with (50 50 50) cells and simpleGrading (1 1 1); Define inlet boundary as patch with face (0 3 2 1) which is the x=-500 face; Define outlet boundary as patch with face (4 5 6 7) which is the x=-50 face; Define upperWall boundary as wall with two faces (0 1 2 3) and (4 7 6 5); Define lowerWall boundary as wall with two faces (1 0 7 4) and (3 2 6 5); Define leftWall boundary as wall with face (0 4 5 1); Define rightWall boundary as wall with face (3 7 6 2); Remove mergePatchPairs section')]
